data.title: Hyperscale Data Increases Bitcoin Holdings to Over 675 BTC
data.description: Hyperscale Data, a company listed under the New York Stock Exchange, has announced an increase in its Bitcoin holdings to 675.3529 BTC. According to Foresight News, the total market value of these holdings is approximately $53.1 million. The company's wholly-owned subsidiary, Sentinum, holds 608.4272 BTC, while another subsidiary, ACG, acquired 66.9257 BTC from the open market. Hyperscale Data aims to increase the value of Bitcoin on its balance sheet to $100 million.

data.title: LNG Carrier Navigates Strait of Hormuz Amidst Tensions
data.description: An LNG carrier successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz today, marking the first passage since the recent conflict involving Iran and a U.S.-Israeli coalition. According to NS3.AI, this transit highlights the ongoing geopolitical tensions in the region, which is a critical chokepoint for global energy supplies.

data.title: Ether's Price Faces Key Levels Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description:
Ether (ETH) analysts have identified crucial price levels to monitor in the coming weeks, with particular attention on the $2,000 psychological threshold. According to Cointelegraph, a drop below the 200-day simple moving average at $2,220 could signal further declines for Ether. The cryptocurrency encounters significant resistance at $2,400, a level that bulls must reclaim to sustain upward momentum.

Data from TradingView indicates that the ETH/USD pair is trading below $2,300, marking a 5% decrease over the past two days and negating gains made over the weekend. This positions the price between the 100-day exponential moving average at $2,350 and the 100-day simple moving average at $2,220. Analysts suggest that Ether may consolidate within these trend lines for a few more days before making a decisive move. A Telegram trading resource, Technical Crypto Analyst, noted that losing the support trendline at $2,300 could lead to further declines, potentially reaching lower support levels soon. The analyst highlighted two immediate support zones: the $2,200 area, where the 50-day and 100-day SMAs converge, and the psychological level at $2,000.

Fellow analyst Ted Pillows emphasized the importance of the $2,200 support zone for a potential short-term rebound. Below this, a key buy zone lies between $1,800 and $1,750, aligning with the multi-year low reached on February 6. Trader Daan Crypto Trades identified $2,100 as a support level and $2,800 as resistance, noting that ETH has consistently respected these levels over the years. As reported by Cointelegraph, a daily close below the moving averages around $2,200 would shift focus to the next line of defense at $2,000.

For Ether to continue its recovery, it must convert the $2,400 resistance into support, where the realized price currently stands. CryptoQuant analyst CW8900 highlighted the psychological significance of this level, noting that breaking through it would indicate that whales are moving into profitable positions. This shift could strengthen their buying power. Meanwhile, Ether's liquidation map shows that surpassing $2,400 would trigger over $1.94 billion in short liquidations across exchanges, suggesting that a move higher could lead to a sharper upward trend if the recovery continues.

data.title: Oil Prices Surge as WTI Breaks $103 and Brent Tops $105 on Renewed Supply Fears
data.description: Oil prices surged sharply on April 28, with WTI crude breaking through $103 per barrel -- a 4.54% single-day gain -- and Brent crude topping $105 per barrel, up 3.12%, according to Bitget data.

data.title: Hungary's Central Bank Maintains Benchmark Interest Rate at 6.25%
data.description: Hungary's central bank has decided to keep its benchmark interest rate steady at 6.25%, aligning with market expectations. According to Jin10, this decision reflects the bank's ongoing strategy to manage inflation and support economic stability. The rate has remained unchanged as the bank assesses the current economic conditions and monitors global financial trends. This move is part of a broader effort to ensure sustainable growth while addressing potential economic challenges.

data.title: Poland Anticipates Increased Cyber Threats Amid AI Advancements
data.description: Poland is preparing for an escalation in cyber threats as advanced AI tools become more widespread, according to a senior government official. Bloomberg posted on X that the country is currently facing a growing number of cyberattacks originating from Russia. The official emphasized the need for heightened vigilance and enhanced cybersecurity measures to counter these evolving threats. As AI technology continues to develop, the potential for more sophisticated cyberattacks increases, posing significant challenges for national security. Poland is actively working to strengthen its defenses against these online threats, recognizing the critical importance of cybersecurity in the digital age.

data.title: Hyperscale Data, a publicly traded company, increased its holdings by 675 bitcoins, aiming to reach $100 million.
data.description: Hyperscale Data, a NYSE-listed company, announced that its Bitcoin treasury holdings have increased to 675,3529 Bitcoins, with a total market value of approximately $53.1 million. Of these, its wholly-owned subsidiary Sentinum holds 608,4272 Bitcoins, while another subsidiary, ACG, purchased 66,9257 Bitcoins from the open market. Hyperscale Data aims to increase the value of its Bitcoin holdings on its balance sheet to $100 million.

data.title: EXIO Group and Finoverse AI Launch AI-Driven Token Planet
data.description: EXIO Group, a provider of comprehensive digital asset solutions, and Finoverse AI, a global technology ecosystem network, have announced the launch of 'Token Planet.' According to Foresight News, this initiative is the world's first AI-driven launchpad specifically designed for early-stage entrepreneurial projects. Token Planet aims to merge traditional private equity investment with blockchain technology to enhance liquidity for early-stage assets.

data.title: Kazakhstan's Central Bank May Cut Interest Rates if Inflation Continues to Slow
data.description: Kazakhstan's central bank has indicated the possibility of reducing interest rates if the current trend of slowing inflation persists. According to Jin10, the bank is closely monitoring inflationary trends and is prepared to adjust its monetary policy accordingly. This move is seen as a response to the ongoing economic conditions and aims to support economic growth while maintaining price stability. The central bank's decision will depend on future inflation data and economic indicators.

data.title: AI TRENDS | China Approves 690 Industry Standards Including AI Deep Synthesis Image Systems
data.description: On April 28, China's Ministry of Industry and Information Technology approved 690 industry standards, including the 'Technical Specifications for AI Deep Synthesis Image Systems.' According to Jin10, these standards cover various sectors: 56 in the chemical industry, 11 in petrochemicals, 81 in ferrous metallurgy, 61 in non-ferrous metals, 4 in the gold industry, 61 in building materials, 9 in rare earths, 120 in machinery, 46 in the automotive sector, 4 in light industry, 92 in textiles, 1 in military civilian products, 35 in electronics, and 109 in communications. Additionally, a modification to the 'Guidelines for the Classification and Filing of Telecom and Internet Network Security Protection' was approved as a communication industry standard. Furthermore, 28 foreign language versions of industry standards were approved, including 2 in non-ferrous metals, 1 in rare earths, and 25 in communications. These modifications take effect immediately upon publication.

data.title: Gate Ventures: Tech stocks drive market recovery, crypto assets and investment/financing recover in tandem.
data.description: According to Gate Ventures' latest weekly report, there are signs of a phased recovery in the macro environment. Major stock indices showed divergent performance but were generally upward, and market risk appetite has rebounded. Against this backdrop, the crypto market rebounded in tandem, with BTC rising 6.6% and ETH rising 4.7%, recording net inflows of approximately $823.7 million and $155 million respectively into spot ETFs, indicating a strengthening of capital inflows. The overall market capitalization rose 5.2%, while the market capitalization excluding BTC and ETH increased by 2.6%, indicating that the upward momentum is beginning to spread to a wider range of assets, but the pace remains relatively moderate. In terms of asset and industry dynamics, structural opportunities continue to emerge. The top 30 assets rose by an average of 4.2%. Meanwhile, on-chain and industry-level progress continues, including the ongoing evolution of digital currency infrastructure and asset on-chaining. In terms of investment and financing, 12 transactions were completed last week, with a total disclosed financing amount of approximately $54.89 million, an increase of approximately 31% week-on-week, with funds mainly flowing into the DeFi and infrastructure sectors. Among them, JPYC completed a $17.62 million funding round to advance the construction of the Japanese yen stablecoin infrastructure; 3F completed a $4 million seed round, with investors including Gate Ventures. Against the backdrop of a marginal improvement in the macroeconomic environment, investment and financing activity has rebounded, with funds still focusing on long-term application scenarios and underlying capability building in a volatile environment.

data.title: Oil prices continued to rise, with Brent crude breaking through $105.
data.description: According to Gate data, WTI crude oil broke through $103 per barrel, up 4.54% on the day. Brent crude oil broke through $105 per barrel, up 3.12% on the day.

data.title: Saudi Aramco Extends LPG Delivery Suspension Until End of May
data.description: Saudi Aramco has announced an extension of its suspension on liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) deliveries. According to Odaily, the company will continue the suspension until the end of May. This decision follows previous measures taken by the company regarding LPG distribution.

data.title: General Motors Faces Inflation Pressure on Aluminum, Steel, and Transportation Costs
data.description: General Motors (GM.N) is experiencing inflationary pressures on aluminum, steel, and transportation costs. According to Jin10, the company has noted significant increases in these areas, which are impacting its overall cost structure. The automotive industry is closely monitoring these developments as they could affect production and pricing strategies moving forward.

data.title: Spot silver fell 4% on the day, currently trading at $72.42 per ounce.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold fell more than 2% intraday, last trading at $4,582.05 per ounce. Spot silver fell 4% intraday, currently trading at $72.42 per ounce.

data.title: Market news: Saudi Aramco extends its suspension of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) deliveries until the end of May.
data.description: Market news: Saudi Aramco has extended its suspension of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) deliveries until the end of May. (Jinshi)

data.title: Pharos Launches Mainnet with High Yield RWA Vault
data.description: Pharos has successfully launched its mainnet, featuring a pAlpha High Yield Real-World Asset (RWA) Vault that has already reached its $50 million capacity. According to NS3.AI, the company anticipates more than 50 applications to deploy at launch. The Atlantic testnet, which preceded the mainnet, processed over 4.3 billion transactions across 209 million wallets in less than a year. Co-founder and CEO Wish Wu emphasized that the network is designed to integrate assets, compliance, privacy, and applications within a single environment tailored for real-world assets.

data.title: MARA launched the MARA Foundation to improve Bitcoin security and expand self-custody and educational resources.
data.description: MARA has launched the MARA Foundation to enhance Bitcoin's resilience against security threats, including risks such as quantum computing, while also expanding the accessibility of self-custody and providing educational resources. (Bitcoin News)

data.description: 1. WTI crude oil touched $102/barrel, a new high since April 13; 2. Spot gold fell below the $4,600 mark; 3. Polymarket is undergoing a coordinated upgrade of its exchange stack, expected to end at 20:00; 4. Bitmine just pledged $214 million worth of ETH; 5. Data: Binance's stablecoin inflows exceeded $6 billion in the past two months; 6. Block Inc. increased its holdings by 114.89 Bitcoins, bringing its total holdings to 8,998; 7. Crypto hackers have stolen a total of $17.1 billion in assets over the past decade; 8. Galaxy Digital OTC-linked addresses transferred over 20,000 ETH to exchanges within 24 hours; 9. data.title: Israel approves its first regulated stablecoin pegged to the shekel.
data.description: The Israel Capital Markets Authority has approved Bits of Gold's issuance of BILS, the country's first stablecoin pegged to the shekel. This token is a regulated asset launched by Bits of Gold after a two-year evaluation and pilot program, with support from the Solana Network, Fireblocks, and Ernst & Young. Bits of Gold stated that bringing the shekel onto the blockchain aims to place it alongside currencies already integrated into the blockchain financial system, such as the euro, yen, and Singapore dollar. With the stablecoin market currently exceeding $300 billion, this issuance aims to counter the dominance of dollar-pegged tokens in on-chain payments and uphold digital sovereignty.
